summary:
- **Core topic/purpose:** Colonial Office file (CO 273/670/9) tracking and coordinating **miscellaneous claims against Malayan/Straits Settlements (and related Hong Kong) government bodies** during wartime, including whether claims should be **paid provisionally, refused, or registered for post-war settlement**.  
- **Time period & status:** Mainly **1942â€“1944 correspondence** (file labeled **1943**); file marked **â€śCLOSED UNTIL 1972.â€ť** Context is Japanese occupation/war disruption and inability to verify records until **re-occupation**.  
- **Key actors/organizations:** **Colonial Office (Eastern Dept.)**, **Crown Agents for the Colonies**, **Trading with the Enemy Department**, **Hong Kong & Malayan Government Accountant (Mr. Reed; Hope House, 45 Great Peter Street, London)**, **General Post Office**, **Ministry of War Transport**, **Air Ministry**, banks including **Chartered Bank of India, Australia & China** and **Hongkong & Shanghai Banking Corporation**, plus various firms/claimants (e.g., **Hume Pipe (Far East) Ltd**, **Yuills Ltd**, **Thornycroft Ltd**, **Singapore Cold Storage Co.**).  
- **Major decisions/policies recorded:** Establishment of **registers** for categories of claims to await post-war verification: **(a) commercial claims, (b) goods insured under Malayan War Risks (Goods) Insurance Fund, (c) requisitioned goods/property, (d) refund of deposits**; Crown Agents asked to supply data to Reed for registration (letter signed **J. J. Paskin**, March 1943).  
- **Bank guarantee / Bulk Order Steel Scheme (Hume Pipe):** Hume Pipe sought confirmation that a **bank guarantee** (HK & Shanghai Banking Corp.) tied to the **Bulk Order Steel Scheme** was released; Colonial Office view (Apr 1943) was that **no formal release could be executed** because the guarantee was to the **Straits Settlements Government** and wartime uncertainty/verification problems persisted. Notable figures: **1,803 tons of steel** ordered; guarantee **$45,080.00**.  
- **Malaya Broadcasting Corporation (MBC) diverted cargo & interest:** Extensive handling of **Chartered Bank** claims for MBC goods diverted/requisitioned in **Australia** or landed elsewhere; discussion of whether **interest is automatically due** on unpaid bills and at what rate. Decision/advice recorded that interest is due; **5% p.a. considered too high**, with suggested compromise **3%**. Notable amounts include **U.S.$139.58** interest reduced to **U.S.$83.75**, and further bills **U.S.$8,573.97**, **U.S.$1,291.22**, **U.S.$1,346.09**; some payments proposed **subject to indemnity** given improbability of prior payment in Singapore.  
- **Postal parcels opened/requisitioned at Singapore (Feb 1942):** GPO forwarded Malayan Post Office account that **four UKâ†’Hong Kong parcel mails (Nos. 29â€“32 of 1941)** were opened at Singapore; **790 of 1,374 parcels requisitioned**, **584 returned to London in 67 bags**. Disposal list includes **Command Welfare Officer (526)**, **Base Ordnance Depot (64)**, **Civil Medical Dept (63)**, **Singapore Harbour Board (27)**, etc. Colonial Office proposed claimants lodge claims with **Crown Agents**; later view was to treat such claims on a **â€śrequisition basisâ€ť** and forward for **registration**, with a possible exception for **Campbell Bros. & Carter Ltd.** if not already met.  
- **Other notable claim events:**  
  - **Air passage costs** charged to Malayan funds: authority sought/approved to pay **ÂŁ199 6s 0d** (Mr. **C.A. Larkin**, Takoradiâ†’Singapore, May 1941) and **ÂŁ451 16s 0d** (Messrs **Moore & Tyrrell**, Durbanâ†’Singapore, Aug 1941; MBC).  
  - **Kedah hospital construction claim:** via High Commissioner in Canberra (Oct 1943) forwarding **L.T.A. Robb** claim for **Straits $88,240** for a new ward at **Alor Star Hospital**, Kedah; suggested the claim would likely remain **in abeyance until end of war** but warranted a courtesy reply.  
  - **Thornycroft (Singapore) demolition/denial evidence:** Major **G. Morgan** report (July 1943) on wartime â€śdenialâ€ť actions at **Tanjong Rhu shipyard**, noting uncertainty whether premises were ultimately burned by military due to later orders/cut communications.
